title: Bacterial Plating Quantification
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"emdk","text":"This protocol describes how to serially dilute and plate cultured bacteria in suspension for quantification.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3035k","text":"Labcoat and gloves must be worn at all times.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dqtph","text":"Always wipe entire work area and all tools with 70% ethanol or 10% bleach as part of clean-up.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"d547p","text":"When working with dangerous or pathogenic bacterial strains such as E. coli O157:H7, be extra cautious to avoid spreading the bacteria or getting it on your skin.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":68,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"93ck","text":"Gloves","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5b8sc","text":"Stock bacteria culture","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"111vm","text":"Pipette and tips","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dsihc","text":"2 mL centrifuge tubes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8has4","text":"DI water","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"anjq3","text":"Agar plate (see separate protocol for how to make agar plates)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8n4t2","text":"Inoculation loop","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gaa9","text":"Parafilm","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"18gvi","text":"70% ethanol (EtOH) or 10% bleach solution","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Transfer 100 µL of stock solution to 2 mL centrifuge tube.
Add 900 µL DI water to the same centrifuge tube to make a dilution that is one-tenth the concentration of the stock solution.
Using 100 µL of the dilution you just made, make another dilution that is one-tenth the concentration of the second solution.
Repeat 6 times until you have a solution that is diluted by 10 6 . How to perform serial dilutions. X represents the number of 10-fold dilutions performed at each point.
Choose a dilution that you feel is appropriate and dispense 100 µL of that dilution on agar plate.

Evenly spread the cell suspension over the agarose gel with inoculation loop. This may be different from how you have streaked before since this is for quantification, not identification. We are wanting the solution evenly spread so that we can count how many colonies are formed after incubation.
Wrap the agar plate's edge in parafilm. If you have not worked with Parafilm, ask a TA for help in wrapping your plate.
Wait 2 minutes, then flip the plate upside-down for incubation.
Label the plate with: Bacterial strain Date Dilution used (10^x) Initials
Count the number of colonies formed. You can use the image of the plate. This can be done by hand or using an image processing software such as ImageJ.
Calculate the original E. Coli concentration using the following equation:
